levels.question: Why did particle count exceed the Grade A alert threshold?
levels.suggestion: Airflow performance around HEPA-FF-L2-07 degraded during the active filling window.

levels.question: Why did airflow performance degrade?
levels.suggestion: The HEPA filter was still within the old 18-month preventive maintenance interval but showed trend deterioration.

levels.question: Why was trend deterioration not acted on earlier?
levels.suggestion: Environmental trend review was not linked to automatic maintenance interval reassessment.

levels.question: Why was maintenance interval reassessment not triggered?
levels.suggestion: SOP-PM-HEPA-001 still defines a fixed interval and does not include trend-based shortening criteria.

levels.question: Why does the SOP not include trend-based criteria?
levels.suggestion: The SOP was not updated after internal quality guidance changed the recommended Grade A/B replacement interval.

confirmedRootCause: HEPA filter replacement interval remained at 18 months despite trend evidence supporting 12 months.
